title: camissonia claviformis subsp. peeblesii
intro: Camissonia claviformis subsp. peeblesii, also known as Peebles' cliff-f Bog-star, is a rare, flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its roots and leaves. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues, due to its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve pain. Camissonia claviformis subsp. peeblesii is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the dried roots and leaves in hot water, making it a potential herbal remedy for various ailments.
